diagrams.title: Requirement Engineering Process Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the four phases of requirement engineering in sequence
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the sequential process and outputs of each phase

diagrams.title: Functional vs Non-Functional Requirements
diagrams.description: Two-column comparison showing differences between requirement types
elements: Functional: What system does, Features, Functions, Examples: login, search, process data
Non-Functional: How system performs, Quality, Constraints, Examples: speed, security, reliability
diagrams.purpose: Clear distinction between the two main requirement types with examples

diagrams.title: SRS Quality Attributes (IEEE 830)
diagrams.description: Seven characteristics of good SRS arranged as a checklist
elements: Correct, Unambiguous, Complete, Consistent, Verifiable, Modifiable, Traceable
For each: Definition and why it matters
diagrams.purpose: Visual checklist for reviewing SRS quality

diagrams.title: Stakeholder Categories
diagrams.description: Venn diagram showing different stakeholder groups and their relationships
diagrams.purpose: Understanding who stakeholders are and their overlapping interests

diagrams.title: Requirement Elicitation Techniques
diagrams.description: Five common elicitation methods with icons
diagrams.purpose: Visual reminder of available techniques for gathering requirements
